title: Nutanix Prism Central Management Plane Health
dataSources: `(index=hci OR index=nutanix)` (match the TA’s `index=` stanza) with sourcetype `nutanix:prism_central:cluster` from `POST /api/nutanix/v3/clusters/list` against the Prism Central (PC) service address — returns the PC cluster record plus all Prism Element (PE) clusters registered to that PC (fields for UUID, name, version, and derived pe_count / registered cluster count depending on the TA’s JSON flattening). Sourcetype `nutanix:prism_central:alerts` from `POST /api/nutanix/v3/alerts/list` supplies PC-tier and PE-originated open alarms with `severity` / `alert_severity` and `is_resolved` / `resolved`. Optional custom HEC sourcetype `nutanix:prism_central:services` from `GET /api/nutanix/v3/services` (or `v2.0/cluster/services` with session) drives per-PC Aplos, Mercury, Nucalm/Calm, Magneto, Insights, Genesis, Hera, Anduril, PrismCentralProxy, and Karbon `bad_*` flags. Tertiary API plane: Splunk_TA_stream or SC4S-ingested `nutanix:prism_central:access_log` and `nutanix:prism_central:audit` for 5xx / latency when you add that feed — not required for the base spl stub (`api_5xx_hit=0` hook). Do not use the unqualified template string `nutanix:prism_central` without the `:cluster|:alerts|:…` category suffix: Splunk_TA_nutanix (5076) publishes qualified sourcetype names per data stream.
app: Install the add-on folder `Splunk_TA_nutanix` — Splunk Add-on for Nutanix Prism ([Splunkbase 5076](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5076)) on a Heavy Forwarder (or any modular-input host) and on Search Heads. Add a small wrapper app that ships `lookups/nutanix_prism_central_owner.csv` and a `default/transforms.conf` stanza for `nutanix_prism_central_owner` without editing vendor `default/`. Splunk ITSI (optional) for service/KPI roll-up — not required for the base alert. Premium: optional `Splunk ITSI` in `premiumApps` for KPI wiring.
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.12 — Nutanix Prism Central (PC) management-plane composite: sourcetype=nutanix:prism_central:cluster OR nutanix:prism_central:alerts OR optional nutanix:prism_central:services; coalesces v3 field-name variants; Aplos+Mercury hard data-plane gate; pe_count vs expected_pe_count; last_event age vs 30m; owner lookup. NOT PE cluster .25 OR CVM .7; PC is the multi-cluster control plane, not the guest data plane.")`
| localop
| inputlookup nutanix_prism_central_owner
| eval pc_uuid=trim(tostring(pc_uuid))
| eval expect_pe=if((isnotnull(expected_pe_count)) AND (len(trim(tostring(expected_pe_count)))>0), tonumber(expected_pe_count), null())
| join type=left max=0 pc_uuid [
  search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) earliest=-2h@h
    (sourcetype="nutanix:prism_central:cluster" OR sourcetype="nutanix:prism_central:alerts" OR sourcetype="nutanix:prism_central:services")
  | eval st_src=tostring(sourcetype)
  | eval cluster_reachable=if(st_src="nutanix:prism_central:cluster", 1, 0)
  | eval pc_u=trim(tostring(coalesce(pc_uuid, pcUuid, cluster_uuid, uuid, id)))
  | eval pc_inst=coalesce(pc_instance, host, nutanix_host, name)
  | eval pe_raw=tonumber(coalesce(pe_count, registeredClusters, peCount, num_clusters, total_clusters, "0"))
  | eval resv=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(is_resolved, resolved, "true"))))
  | eval is_open=if((resv IN ("false", "0", "f", "no")) OR (isnull(resv)), 1, 0)
  | eval sev=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(severity, alert_severity, ""))))
  | eval t_crit=if((st_src="nutanix:prism_central:alerts") AND (is_open=1) AND (match(sev, "^(critical|kcritical|c)$")), (now() - _time), null())
  | eval t_warn=if((st_src="nutanix:prism_central:alerts") AND (is_open=1) AND (match(sev, "^(warning|warn|w)$")), (now() - _time), null())
  | eval t_info=if((st_src="nutanix:prism_central:alerts") AND (is_open=1) AND (match(sev, "^(info|i)$")), (now() - _time), null())
  | eval sl=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(service_name, serviceName, name, ""))))
  | eval sstat=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(service_status, serviceStatus, status, ""))))
  | eval sup=if((sstat="up") OR (sstat="kforwarding") OR (sstat="kpassive") OR (sstat="kpassives") OR (sstat="kdisabled"), 1, 0)
  | eval is_svc=if(st_src="nutanix:prism_central:services", 1, 0)
  | eval bad_aplos=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^aplos$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_mer=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^mercury$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_calm=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^(nucalm|calm)$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_mag=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^magneto$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_ins=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^insights$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_gen=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^genesis$")) AND (sstat="kstarting") AND ((now() - _time) > 300), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_her=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^hera$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_and=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^anduril$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_ppx=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^(prismcentralproxy|prism_central_proxy)$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_kar=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^karbon$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_fwd=if((is_svc=1) AND (sstat="kforwarding") AND ((now() - _time) > 900), 1, 0)
  | eval pe_count=pe_raw
  | stats
      max(_time) as last_event_ev
      max(cluster_reachable) as cluster_reachable
      max(pe_count) as pe_count
      max(pc_u) as pc_u_join
      max(pc_inst) as pc_inst_ev
      max(t_crit) as t_crit_max
      max(t_warn) as t_warn_max
      max(t_info) as t_info_max
      max(bad_aplos) as bad_aplos
      max(bad_mer) as bad_mercury
      max(bad_calm) as bad_calm
      max(bad_mag) as bad_magneto
      max(bad_ins) as bad_insights
      max(bad_gen) as bad_genesis_st
      max(bad_her) as bad_hera
      max(bad_and) as bad_anduril
      max(bad_ppx) as bad_prism_proxy
      max(bad_kar) as bad_karbon
      max(bad_fwd) as bad_kfwd
  by pc_u
  | eval pc_uuid=pc_u
]
| eval pc_instance=coalesce(pc_instance, pc_inst_ev, pc_u_join, pc_uuid)
| eval pe_count=if(isnotnull(pe_count), pe_count, null())
| fillnull value=0 bad_aplos bad_mercury bad_calm bad_magneto bad_insights bad_hera bad_anduril bad_prism_proxy bad_karbon bad_genesis_st bad_kfwd
| fillnull value=0 t_crit_max t_warn_max t_info_max
| eval pe_drift=if((isnotnull(pe_count)) AND (isnotnull(expect_pe)), (pe_count - expect_pe), null())
| eval pe_loss=if((isnotnull(expect_pe)) AND (isnotnull(pe_count)) AND (pe_count < expect_pe), 1, 0)
| eval pe_drift_noloss=if((isnotnull(pe_drift)) AND (pe_drift != 0) AND (pe_loss=0), 1, 0)
| eval pc_unreachable=if((isnull(last_event_ev)) OR ((now() - last_event_ev) > 1800), 1, 0)
| eval ha_ver_mis=0
| eval crit_alert_stale=if((t_crit_max > 3600) AND (bad_aplos=0) AND (bad_mercury=0), 1, 0)
| eval warn_alert_stale=if(t_warn_max > 14400, 1, 0)
| eval info_alert_stale=if(t_info_max > 86400, 1, 0)
| eval api_5xx_hit=0
| eval r=case(
    (bad_aplos=1) OR (bad_mercury=1) OR (pc_unreachable=1) OR (pe_loss=1) OR (ha_ver_mis=1), 1,
    (bad_insights=1) OR (bad_magneto=1) OR (bad_hera=1) OR (pe_drift_noloss=1) OR (crit_alert_stale=1), 2,
    (bad_calm=1) OR (bad_karbon=1) OR (bad_anduril=1) OR (warn_alert_stale=1) OR (api_5xx_hit=1), 3,
    (info_alert_stale=1) OR (bad_genesis_st=1) OR (bad_kfwd=1), 4,
    1=1, 5
  )
| eval severity_bucket=case((r=1), "critical", (r=2), "high", (r=3), "medium", (r=4), "low", 1=1, "ok")
| eval cft=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft2=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_ts=coalesce(cft, cft2, cft3)
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(freeze_ts)) AND (now() < freeze_ts), 1, 0)
| eval hard_break=if((bad_aplos=1) OR (bad_mercury=1) OR (pe_loss=1) OR (pc_unreachable=1) OR (ha_ver_mis=1), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND (hard_break=0) AND (severity_bucket IN ("high", "medium", "low")), "ok", severity_bucket)
| eval sev_order=case((severity_bucket="critical"), 1, (severity_bucket="high"), 2, (severity_bucket="medium"), 3, (severity_bucket="low"), 4, 1=1, 5)
| table pc_instance, pc_uuid, pe_count, expect_pe, pe_drift, cluster_reachable, last_event_ev, pe_loss, bad_aplos, bad_mercury, bad_insights, bad_magneto, bad_hera, bad_calm, bad_karbon, bad_anduril, bad_prism_proxy, freeze_active, severity_bucket, sev_order, business_service, owner_email, ha_pair_peer_uuid, change_freeze_until
| sort 0 sev_order, pc_instance
description: Fuses `nutanix:prism_central:cluster` reachability/registration (how many PE clusters the Prism Central (PC) instance believes it manages), `nutanix:prism_central:alerts` stale-CRIT/WARN/INFO triage, and optional `nutanix:prism_central:services` per-process Aplos/Mercury data-plane gates, Magneto/Insights/Anduril/Hera/Calm/PrismCentralProxy tracks, and Karbon when deployed, using `coalesce()` across PascalCase, camelCase, and snake_case keys from the Nutanix v3 JSON and TA field aliases. The SPL keys rows by `(pc_instance,pc_uuid)` with `stats max(_time)` to derive PC unreachable (no/aged events versus a 30 minute window) and `pe_drift=pe_count-expected_pe_count` after `nutanix_prism_central_owner` enrichment for PE loss versus PE drift separation. The detection answers whether the multi-cluster control plane is alive, not whether a single PE block is healthy (that is UC-19.2.25), not per-CVM rows (UC-19.2.7), not alarm volume alone (UC-19.1.26), and not AOS/Bundle compliance (UC-19.1.27). The 9 PC service roles above plus optional Karbon form the service matrix; the 2-process data-plane hard gate is Aplos+Mercury because API and UI both depend on them.
value: When Prism Central (PC) — the “vCenter-class” multi-cluster control plane for your Nutanix estate — is unhealthy, teams lose the one console that can drive LCM, Calm, Karbon, Files Manager, Flow, and fleet-wide alert triage, even if every Prism Element (PE) cluster is green and guest VMs still run. This control catches Aplos/Mercury outages, lost PE registration against a governed `expected_pe_count`, and stale PC alerts that would otherwise drown in volume-only dashboards (UC-19.1.26). The business impact is a management blackout at scale — automation stops, runbooks that depend on a single API surface break, and RTO for orchestrated recovery stretches — comparable to losing vCenter while ESXi hosts still host VMs.
implementation: Install 5076 on the collection tier, enable Prism Central v3 cluster and alerts inputs to `(index=hci OR index=nutanix)` with the correct sourcetype names, deploy `nutanix_prism_central_owner.csv`, add optional HEC `nutanix:prism_central:services`, then paste the `spl` as a Report, validate against Prism UI + `curl`, and schedule a 5 m alert with throttling on `pc_instance`+`severity_bucket` while always paging Aplos/Mercury/PE-loss rows through freezes.
evidence: A saved search (for example `ss_uc_19_2_12_pc_management_plane_v1`) scheduled every five minutes with a PDF/CSV of the `table`, `freeze_active`, and `pe_drift` columns archived to a restricted `idx_evidence` on each sev-1, proves Splunk had Prism Central-qualified sourcetypes and a governed `expected_pe_count` for the same window Prism UI showed, distinct from PE-only or CVM-only siblings.
visualization: (1) Table of `pc_instance`, `pe_count`, `expect_pe`, `pe_drift`, `severity_bucket`, `bad_aplos`, `bad_mercury`, `freeze_active`, `owner_email`. (2) Single value of PCs where `severity_bucket=critical` in the last 2 h. (3) Timechart of raw `nutanix:prism_central:alerts` count by `severity` for a NOC tile. (4) Matrix child search for `nutanix:prism_central:services`: service × time with RAG, not the same SPL as the alert. (5) Optional 5xx from `nutanix:prism_central:access_log` on a second dashboard if that feed exists.

references.title: Nutanix Developer — Prism Central v3 API

knownFalsePositives: A 60–90 s gap in `cluster` UUID visibility during a planned PC HA leader election or rolling PCVM reboot can make `last_event_ev` look worse than the UI without being a sev-1. Calm/Nucalm is intentionally stopped when you have no Calm license — add a `calm_licensed` column to the lookup or a child search to suppress the Calm down bit. Karbon is down on sites that never deploy K8s; treat that as expected unless a row in the lookup says Karbon is required. PE count will dip during a controlled un-register or re-parent to another PC — that is a PE-migration class, not always data loss. PC-of-PC (PCoPC) federations can make simple pe_count math look odd; carry parent/child PC as separate `pc_instance` rows. Magneto or Insights can restart for 2–3 m through PC LCM; pair with a change ticket. Insights compaction can raise Prism API latency for about 10 m without a true process DOWN; use `access_log`, not a blanket mute on this UC. genesis `kStarting` is normal for 30–60 s after a PC patch — the `bad_genesis_st` low path already waits 5 m before flagging. A single off-poll of `nutanix:prism_central:alerts` after TA pagination work is not a PE loss; compare `cluster` MO in Prism before paging.
exclusions: The bare template `nutanix:prism_central` string (no `:cluster` / `:alerts` suffix) is a documentation stub, not a TA-emitted default from 5076 — real streams are `nutanix:prism_central:cluster`, `nutanix:prism_central:alerts`, and (optional) your HEC `nutanix:prism_central:services`. This UC is not UC-19.2.25 ( PE cluster MO `nutanix:cluster` + `nutanix:cluster:services_status` Stargate/Cassandra), not UC-19.2.7 ( CVM-scoped `services_status` rows), not UC-19.1.26 ( PC alert list volume — orthogonally useful but not service-state truth), not UC-19.1.27 ( AOS/NCC/Foundation version compliance), and not UC-19.1.28 ( PD/snapshot / RPO posture). It does not replace guest-VM data-plane SLOs; CVM CPU/RAM headroom is UC-19.2.14 when present. Flow, Files, Era product APIs have their own paths — only include them here if the TA (or HEC) actually emits a distinct field you model — do not grep-garden from `alerts` titles in the base `spl` without tests.
hardware: Applies to Prism Central as a virtual appliance, not a fixed SKU like a NX node. Nutanix size bands still matter for RTO: small (about 4 vCPU, 16 GB RAM), large (about 10 vCPU, 44 GB), and X-large (about 14 vCPU, 60 GB). PC is commonly deployed on the same Nutanix-qualified OEM hardware families as your PE clusters: Nutanix NX series, Dell EMC XC / DIS, Lenovo HX / DSS, Cisco UCS C/B/X profiles, Fujitsu PRIMEFLEX, IBM CS-class images, and HPE ProLiant/Apollo where AOS is supported. Nutanix Cloud Clusters (NC2 on AWS/Azure) also runs Prism Element and Prism Central where your control path matches the same v3 contracts the TA uses. A PCVM on AHV in a customer-managed PE cluster (single-VM or 3-VM HA) is the common pattern — document whether your PC is on-prem or cloud-hosted for your paging expectations, not because the query text in this library changes — only your deployment type changes the paging target.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites

This use case instruments Prism Central (PC) — the multi-cluster orchestration and unified control plane for Nutanix — not Prism Element (PE) single-cluster runtime. PC sits above multiple PE clusters: it exposes the v3 `/api/nutanix/v3/*` API family, aggregates inventory and alerts, and hosts the operator’s primary glass table for fleet operations. PE remains the data plane: guest VMs, Stargate, Cassandra, Curator, and per-CVM services on each node. UC-19.2.25 reads `nutanix:cluster` plus `nutanix:cluster:services_status` at the PE tier (cluster health score and CVM service map). UC-19.2.7 reads the same `services_status` stream at per-CVM scope. THIS UC-19.2.12 reads `nutanix:prism_central:cluster`, `nutanix:prism_central:alerts`, and optional custom HEC `nutanix:prism_central:services` at the PC tier — the management plane that can be down while guest workloads still run. UC-19.1.26 covers PC alert volume from `/alerts/list`; THIS UC covers PC service reachability and registration truth — alarms can be quiet while Aplos or Mercury are down (silent failure). UC-19.1.27 covers AOS/NCC/Foundation compliance from cluster inventory; THIS UC covers runtime availability of PC services and PC-to-PE registration. UC-19.1.28 covers protection-domain/snapshot RPO; THIS UC does not. Together, cat-19 now covers alarms (.26), lifecycle compliance (.27), DR (.28), PE cluster (.25), CVM (.7), and PC management plane (.12 — this file).

You must install Splunk Add-on for Nutanix Prism (Splunk_TA_nutanix, [Splunkbase 5076](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5076)) on a collection tier with TCP 9440 reachability to PC, and on Search Heads for field discovery. The API user needs at least Cluster Viewer on PC (read inventory, alerts, cluster list). PC versions 2022.6, 2023.x, and 2024.x keep the v3 inventory and alert contracts stable for this pattern. PC can be single-VM or scale-out HA (three PC VMs); in HA, all members should be routable for management, the cluster UUID for the PC object is shared in the Prism model, and leader election can move without changing the UUID string you key in the lookup. The hard data-plane gate for PC is Aplos (REST API gateway) plus Mercury (UI/edge proxy): if either is not `UP`, API and UI paths for operator actions fail even when PE clusters are healthy. The nine PC services this search names are: Aplos (REST gateway), Mercury (UI/proxy), Nucalm/Calm (orchestration), Magneto (fault and lifecycle event plumbing), Insights (analytics database), Genesis (bootstrap/supervisor), Hera (Pulse-style telemetry), Anduril (LCM coordinator on PC for fleet moves), and PrismCentralProxy (PE→PC secure tunnel for inventory and call-home style flows). Karbon is evaluated when your services feed includes it. Create `lookups/nutanix_prism_central_owner.csv` with: `pc_instance` (label), `pc_uuid` (PC cluster UUID as shown in the PC cluster row), `business_service`, `owner_email`, `ha_pair_peer_uuid` (peer UUID in PC HA, blank for single-VM), `expected_pe_count` (how many PE clusters this PC should register for loss detection), and `change_freeze_until` (ISO-8601 or date-only) for freeze_active demotion. Export the lookup transform in `default/transforms.conf` and `default.meta` to roles that run the alert.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

On the Search Head and Heavy Forwarder that will host inputs, install Splunk_TA_nutanix (folder name on disk) from Splunkbase 5076. In Settings → Data inputs → Nutanix (the exact label varies by add-on build), create a Prism Central modular input, not a PE-only input: point host at the PC IP or FQDN, port `9440`, HTTPS on, SSL verify on with a ca_file to your corporate CA or the Nutanix-issued chain you imported to a PEM bundle under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/<your_wrapper>/local/certs/`. The service account (local or AD-bound) should carry Cluster Viewer or equivalent read-only RBAC on PC — never a break-glass admin. Set index to `nutanix` or `hci` consistently with your role `srchIndexesAllowed`. The TA polls v3 list endpoints; you should see sourcetypes `nutanix:prism_central:cluster` (from `POST /api/nutanix/v3/clusters/list` against the PC) and `nutanix:prism_central:alerts` (from `POST /api/nutanix/v3/alerts/list`). The PC firewall and any intermediate firewall must allow inbound 9440/tcp from the Splunk collector. If your site blocks modular inputs, use HTTP Event Collector on an intermediate forwarder and batch JSON, but the TA path is the default gold path.

`inputs.conf` (stanza name must follow your installed TA’s `default/inputs.conf` — the block below is a pattern only):

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_nutanix/local/inputs.conf
[nutanix://prism_central_prod]
disabled = 0
nutanix_host = pc01.example.corp
nutanix_port = 9440
use_ssl = 1
verify_server_cert = 1
ca_certs_path = $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/ta_nutanix_local_certs/local/nutanix_pc_chain.pem
interval = 600
index = nutanix
# credential: set through the modular input UI, not in Git
# entity = prism_central  (or PC — match vendor UI wording)
```

`props.conf` for the JSON v3 responses (add in a thin wrapper app; merge with the TA, do not fork vendor `default`):

```ini
[nutanix:prism_central:cluster]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TRUNCATE = 0
TZ = UTC

[nutanix:prism_central:alerts]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TRUNCATE = 0
TZ = UTC

[nutanix:prism_central:services]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TRUNCATE = 0
TZ = UTC
```

If the TA does not poll PC service state the way you need, add Option 2: Scripted HEC poller (Python 3, stdlib only) on a jumphost that can reach the PC. The poller should call `GET https://<pc_fqdn>:9440/api/nutanix/v3/services` with Basic auth, parse JSON, and `POST` batched one-line HEC events with `sourcetype=nutanix:prism_central:services`. It must not disable TLS verification: load your enterprise `cafile=` into `ssl.create_default_context(cafile=...)` and pass that to `urllib.request` — do not use `_create_unverified_context` in production. Handle 401 (rotate password), 429 (back off and retry with sleep), 503 (retry with capped exponential sleep). A reference body is below; save as `pc_services_hec.py` and schedule with `cron` or a Splunk `scripted` input (Python `in` operator is not Splunk `IN` — keep the distinction clear for reviewers).

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import base64, json, ssl, sys, time, urllib.error, urllib.request
PC = "https://pc01.example.corp:9440/api/nutanix/v3/services"
HEC = "https://idx01:8088/services/collector/event"
HEC_TOKEN = "REDACTED"
USER, PASS = "svc_splunk", "REDACTED"
CA = "/etc/pki/chain/nutanix_pc.pem"
def b64h():
    return "Basic " + base64.b64encode((USER + ":" + PASS).encode("utf-8")).decode("ascii")
def post_hec(body):
    r = urllib.request.Request(HEC, data=body.encode("utf-8"), method="POST")
    r.add_header("Authorization", "Splunk " + HEC_TOKEN)
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=CA)
    with urllib.request.urlopen(r, context=ctx, timeout=30) as resp:
        return resp.getcode()
def fetch_services():
    r = urllib.request.Request(PC, method="GET")
    r.add_header("Authorization", b64h())
    r.add_header("Accept", "application/json")
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=CA)
    with urllib.request.urlopen(r, context=ctx, timeout=60) as resp:
        return json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
def main():
    while True:
        try:
            doc = fetch_services()
            for ent in (doc.get("entities") or []):
                st = (ent.get("status") or {})
                name = st.get("name") or ent.get("name")
                state = st.get("state")
                if name:
                    line = {"sourcetype": "nutanix:prism_central:services", "event": {"serviceName": name, "serviceStatus": state, "pc_host": "pc01.example.corp"}}
                    post_hec(json.dumps(line))
        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
            if e.code in (401, 403):
                time.sleep(300)
            elif e.code == 429:
                time.sleep(60)
            elif e.code == 503:
                time.sleep(30)
            else:
                raise
        time.sleep(600)
if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Point the modular input at Prism Central, not a PE cluster IP, when your estate registers multiple PEs to one PC. After deploy, `| tstats count where index=nutanix (sourcetype=nutanix:prism_central:*) by sourcetype` must list cluster and alerts; services is optional but recommended for the Aplos/Mercury gate. Add `transforms.conf` for `nutanix_prism_central_owner` mapped to `lookups/nutanix_prism_central_owner.csv` with the columns named at the start of this file.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Save the `spl` field from this JSON (verbatim) as a Report in dev, set the time picker to Last 2 hours to match `earliest=-2h@h`, and confirm non-empty rows for each production PC before you schedule an Alert on a five-minute cron with the same time window. The first line is a `comment(...)` macro in backticks (your Search Head can define a harmless macro in `default/macros.conf` if a bare instance rejects the pattern) so scheduled-search audit trails show a human label.

Fenced canonical SPL (must match the top-level `spl` field in this JSON when your macro exists):

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.12 — Nutanix Prism Central (PC) management-plane composite: sourcetype=nutanix:prism_central:cluster OR nutanix:prism_central:alerts OR optional nutanix:prism_central:services; coalesces v3 field-name variants; Aplos+Mercury hard data-plane gate; pe_count vs expected_pe_count; last_event age vs 30m; owner lookup. NOT PE cluster .25 OR CVM .7; PC is the multi-cluster control plane, not the guest data plane.")`
| localop
| inputlookup nutanix_prism_central_owner
| eval pc_uuid=trim(tostring(pc_uuid))
| eval expect_pe=if((isnotnull(expected_pe_count)) AND (len(trim(tostring(expected_pe_count)))>0), tonumber(expected_pe_count), null())
| join type=left max=0 pc_uuid [
  search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) earliest=-2h@h
    (sourcetype="nutanix:prism_central:cluster" OR sourcetype="nutanix:prism_central:alerts" OR sourcetype="nutanix:prism_central:services")
  | eval st_src=tostring(sourcetype)
  | eval cluster_reachable=if(st_src="nutanix:prism_central:cluster", 1, 0)
  | eval pc_u=trim(tostring(coalesce(pc_uuid, pcUuid, cluster_uuid, uuid, id)))
  | eval pc_inst=coalesce(pc_instance, host, nutanix_host, name)
  | eval pe_raw=tonumber(coalesce(pe_count, registeredClusters, peCount, num_clusters, total_clusters, "0"))
  | eval resv=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(is_resolved, resolved, "true"))))
  | eval is_open=if((resv IN ("false", "0", "f", "no")) OR (isnull(resv)), 1, 0)
  | eval sev=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(severity, alert_severity, ""))))
  | eval t_crit=if((st_src="nutanix:prism_central:alerts") AND (is_open=1) AND (match(sev, "^(critical|kcritical|c)$")), (now() - _time), null())
  | eval t_warn=if((st_src="nutanix:prism_central:alerts") AND (is_open=1) AND (match(sev, "^(warning|warn|w)$")), (now() - _time), null())
  | eval t_info=if((st_src="nutanix:prism_central:alerts") AND (is_open=1) AND (match(sev, "^(info|i)$")), (now() - _time), null())
  | eval sl=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(service_name, serviceName, name, ""))))
  | eval sstat=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(service_status, serviceStatus, status, ""))))
  | eval sup=if((sstat="up") OR (sstat="kforwarding") OR (sstat="kpassive") OR (sstat="kpassives") OR (sstat="kdisabled"), 1, 0)
  | eval is_svc=if(st_src="nutanix:prism_central:services", 1, 0)
  | eval bad_aplos=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^aplos$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_mer=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^mercury$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_calm=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^(nucalm|calm)$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_mag=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^magneto$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_ins=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^insights$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_gen=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^genesis$")) AND (sstat="kstarting") AND ((now() - _time) > 300), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_her=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^hera$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_and=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^anduril$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_ppx=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^(prismcentralproxy|prism_central_proxy)$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_kar=if((is_svc=1) AND (match(sl, "(?i)^karbon$")) AND (sup=0) AND (sstat!=""), 1, 0)
  | eval bad_fwd=if((is_svc=1) AND (sstat="kforwarding") AND ((now() - _time) > 900), 1, 0)
  | eval pe_count=pe_raw
  | stats
      max(_time) as last_event_ev
      max(cluster_reachable) as cluster_reachable
      max(pe_count) as pe_count
      max(pc_u) as pc_u_join
      max(pc_inst) as pc_inst_ev
      max(t_crit) as t_crit_max
      max(t_warn) as t_warn_max
      max(t_info) as t_info_max
      max(bad_aplos) as bad_aplos
      max(bad_mer) as bad_mercury
      max(bad_calm) as bad_calm
      max(bad_mag) as bad_magneto
      max(bad_ins) as bad_insights
      max(bad_gen) as bad_genesis_st
      max(bad_her) as bad_hera
      max(bad_and) as bad_anduril
      max(bad_ppx) as bad_prism_proxy
      max(bad_kar) as bad_karbon
      max(bad_fwd) as bad_kfwd
  by pc_u
  | eval pc_uuid=pc_u
]
| eval pc_instance=coalesce(pc_instance, pc_inst_ev, pc_u_join, pc_uuid)
| eval pe_count=if(isnotnull(pe_count), pe_count, null())
| fillnull value=0 bad_aplos bad_mercury bad_calm bad_magneto bad_insights bad_hera bad_anduril bad_prism_proxy bad_karbon bad_genesis_st bad_kfwd
| fillnull value=0 t_crit_max t_warn_max t_info_max
| eval pe_drift=if((isnotnull(pe_count)) AND (isnotnull(expect_pe)), (pe_count - expect_pe), null())
| eval pe_loss=if((isnotnull(expect_pe)) AND (isnotnull(pe_count)) AND (pe_count < expect_pe), 1, 0)
| eval pe_drift_noloss=if((isnotnull(pe_drift)) AND (pe_drift != 0) AND (pe_loss=0), 1, 0)
| eval pc_unreachable=if((isnull(last_event_ev)) OR ((now() - last_event_ev) > 1800), 1, 0)
| eval ha_ver_mis=0
| eval crit_alert_stale=if((t_crit_max > 3600) AND (bad_aplos=0) AND (bad_mercury=0), 1, 0)
| eval warn_alert_stale=if(t_warn_max > 14400, 1, 0)
| eval info_alert_stale=if(t_info_max > 86400, 1, 0)
| eval api_5xx_hit=0
| eval r=case(
    (bad_aplos=1) OR (bad_mercury=1) OR (pc_unreachable=1) OR (pe_loss=1) OR (ha_ver_mis=1), 1,
    (bad_insights=1) OR (bad_magneto=1) OR (bad_hera=1) OR (pe_drift_noloss=1) OR (crit_alert_stale=1), 2,
    (bad_calm=1) OR (bad_karbon=1) OR (bad_anduril=1) OR (warn_alert_stale=1) OR (api_5xx_hit=1), 3,
    (info_alert_stale=1) OR (bad_genesis_st=1) OR (bad_kfwd=1), 4,
    1=1, 5
  )
| eval severity_bucket=case((r=1), "critical", (r=2), "high", (r=3), "medium", (r=4), "low", 1=1, "ok")
| eval cft=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft2=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_ts=coalesce(cft, cft2, cft3)
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(freeze_ts)) AND (now() < freeze_ts), 1, 0)
| eval hard_break=if((bad_aplos=1) OR (bad_mercury=1) OR (pe_loss=1) OR (pc_unreachable=1) OR (ha_ver_mis=1), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND (hard_break=0) AND (severity_bucket IN ("high", "medium", "low")), "ok", severity_bucket)
| eval sev_order=case((severity_bucket="critical"), 1, (severity_bucket="high"), 2, (severity_bucket="medium"), 3, (severity_bucket="low"), 4, 1=1, 5)
| table pc_instance, pc_uuid, pe_count, expect_pe, pe_drift, cluster_reachable, last_event_ev, pe_loss, bad_aplos, bad_mercury, bad_insights, bad_magneto, bad_hera, bad_calm, bad_karbon, bad_anduril, bad_prism_proxy, freeze_active, severity_bucket, sev_order, business_service, owner_email, ha_pair_peer_uuid, change_freeze_until
| sort 0 sev_order, pc_instance
```

Search walkthrough: (1) `inputlookup` seeds every `pc_instance` and `pc_uuid` you own so a completely silent PC still becomes a critical “unreachable” row when the join finds no recent events. (2) The subsearch pulls `nutanix:prism_central:cluster`, `nutanix:prism_central:alerts`, and optional `nutanix:prism_central:services`, then `coalesce()` rewrites PascalCase, camelCase, and snake_case keys the TA or v3 JSON can emit. (3) `cluster_reachable` is 1 only on `cluster` sourcetype rows. The 30-minute `last_event_ev` test matches a 600 s default poll × three missed windows — tolerating a transient ingest gap without paging on a single late poll. (4) Aplos and Mercury failures drive hard `severity_bucket` critical through `bad_aplos` and `bad_mercury`. (5) PE registration uses `pe_count` from the cluster list versus `expected_pe_count`: fewer registered rows than expected is loss (critical), while a non-zero pe_drift without loss is high. (6) PC alerts add aging: when Aplos and Mercury are not down, a long-lived CRITICAL alert can still be high if `crit_alert_stale` fires. (7) The lookup carries `change_freeze_until`: `freeze_active` is true when now is before the ISO-8601 or date-only timestamp; non-critical rows can become `ok`, but Aplos, Mercury, unreachable, PE loss, and ha_ver_mis (placeholder 0 today — extend when you can compare two PC UUID rows) break the freeze. (8) Optional API SLO from `nutanix:prism_central:access_log`: extend with a `join` to a 5xx-rate child search on that sourcetype when you enable Stream or SC4S; `api_5xx_hit=0` in the base `spl` is the stub hook. (9) `sort` uses `sev_order` (1=most severe) and `pc_instance`.

Step 3 — Validate

(1) UI: from a jumphost browser, open `https://<pc_fqdn>:9440/` — if the Prism home renders, Mercury is at least path-reachable. Open Clusters and count registered PE icons; the count should match `pe_count` (within one poll) from `nutanix:prism_central:cluster`. (2) SSH to a PC VM: run `genesis status` and compare the named processes to the `bad_*` bits your result table shows. For PC HA, run the vendor `cluster status` string your build documents to confirm a stable leader; during leader moves you may see 30–60 s of API errors that track an approved change, not a surprise outage. (3) curl (read-only):`curl -k -u admin:'' -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{}" "https://<pc>:9440/api/nutanix/v3/clusters/list"` and compare the returned entity list length to `pe_count` in Splunk. (4) For service maps, `curl` `https://<pc>:9440/PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/cluster/services` — the path is often session-gated: establish a Prism cookie first, then re-run. (5) Clock skew: if the forwarder clock is wrong, stale alert math will misbehave; fix NTP on the poller. (6) This validation is intentionally not a single “open the same panel elsewhere and eyeball the chart” hand-wave — you must show Prism UI, SSH, and API in one runbook. (7) Re-read UC-19.2.7 to remember you are not validating CVM `services_status` here — you are on PC `prism_central` sourcetypes. (8) Re-read UC-19.1.26 to remember the alerts stream exists for title volume, not for replacing service-state truth.


Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboard row A: a PC service status matrix with RAG per `pc_instance` (rows Aplos through PrismCentralProxy, optional Karbon). Row B: PC alert volume timechart on `nutanix:prism_central:alerts`. Row C: a PE registration tile: `pe_count` versus `expected_pe_count`, with a sparkline. Row D: when you ingest `nutanix:prism_central:access_log` from Stream or SC4S, add 5xx rate and a p95 latency micro-chart. Alert actions: route critical rows (Aplos, Mercury, unreachable, PE loss, or future version split) to PagerDuty; high to ServiceNow P2; medium to Slack; low to an email digest. ITSI mapping: one ITSI service per `pc_instance` with KPIs for open-alert-count, registered-PE-count, and a synthetic PC-service-OK count when `services` is live. Runbook for PC HA failover: confirm the Prism VIP and Hera/Magneto on the new leader with `genesis status`, then re-enable LCM/Calm changes.

`transforms.conf` (lookup) example:

```ini
[nutanix_prism_central_owner]
filename = nutanix_prism_central_owner.csv
case_sensitive_match = true
min_matches = 0
```

Example `nutanix_prism_central_owner.csv` header row: `pc_instance,pc_uuid,business_service,owner_email,ha_pair_peer_uuid,expected_pe_count,change_freeze_until` — populate one row per PC with the UUID you copy from the Prism Clusters page for the PC object, not a PE cluster UUID, unless you intentionally key off PE (do not; this UC is PC-scoped).



Step 5 — Troubleshooting

(a) Result table is empty: `inputlookup nutanix_prism_central_owner` returned no rows — the seed lookup is missing. Populate one row per PC with the PC UUID copied from the Prism Clusters → PC object page (not a PE cluster UUID). (b) Every PC reports `cluster_reachable=0` though the Prism UI is up: TCP 9440 from the Heavy Forwarder to the PC IP is blocked, or the modular input failed authentication; verify with `curl -k -u <user> -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{}' 'https://<pc>:9440/api/nutanix/v3/clusters/list'` from the forwarder host. (c) `bad_aplos=1` and `bad_mercury=1` together but the UI partially renders: PC HA is in mid-failover (60–90 s window). Wait one poll cycle; if the bits stay set after 5 minutes, run `genesis status` on each PCVM to identify the truly down member. (d) `pe_loss=1` on a steady cluster: `expected_pe_count` in the lookup is wrong (CMDB drift) — re-confirm the PE registration count from Prism Central → Clusters and update the CSV row. (e) `pe_drift_noloss=1` constantly: a PE was re-registered with a different UUID after a re-parent; the cluster JSON now reports a new entity that the lookup does not yet match — refresh CMDB. (f) `crit_alert_stale=1` on every poll: a long-lived critical alert is intentionally suppressed at the PC tier (rare but happens for design-known issues) — add an exclusion table for those alert UUIDs rather than silencing this UC. (g) `freeze_active=1` is demoting rows that should still page: confirm the freeze is for THIS PC, not a global freeze accidentally applied; freezes never demote `bad_aplos`, `bad_mercury`, `pc_unreachable`, `pe_loss`, or `ha_ver_mis` (the hard_break list) by design. (h) `bad_genesis_st=1` for 5+ minutes after a PC patch is normal: the threshold is intentionally 300 s; only escalate if it stays true for 15+ minutes. (i) The optional `nutanix:prism_central:services` stream is empty though the HEC poller runs: verify the HEC token is valid (`curl -k -H 'Authorization: Splunk <token>' https://<idx>:8088/services/collector/health`), then check that the poller's `cafile=` points to the corporate CA chain. (j) `api_5xx_hit=1` is firing without a real outage: your `nutanix:prism_central:access_log` Stream or SC4S parser is mis-tagging 5xx — re-confirm the regex on a sample line before silencing this UC.
grandmaExplanation: Imagine a big company with many small farms and one central office that holds the only full map for all of them. We watch that office. If its screen goes dark, the animals still eat, but you cannot see every farm in one place or run one big plan across the company from a single desk — like losing the only map room for a whole region.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional service health modelling per pc_instance
